a-c-k.bio: Alpha Centauri Kid (a.c.k.) is an artist and musician whose work spans glitch animation, digital painting, and textured physicals, unified by three recurring motifs: flowers, pianos, and the Muse, the creative force he describes himself as serving.

beeple.bio: Beeple (Mike Winkelmann) has posted a new artwork every day since 2007, a discipline that became Everydays: The First 5000 Days, whose 2021 Christie's sale brought digital art into the auction mainstream. His practice spans the daily sketches, kinetic sculpture, and pointed cultural commentary.

dmitri-cherniak.bio: Dmitri Cherniak's long-form generative practice reinterprets 20th-century minimalism and geometric abstraction through code. Ringers, one thousand algorithmic studies of a string wrapped around pegs, remains one of the defining collections of the Art Blocks Curated program; a single Ringer, The Goose, sold for $6.2M at Sotheby's in 2023.

kim-asendorf.bio: Kim Asendorf has been working with code since the net art era; his 2010 open-source pixel-sorting algorithm became a foundational tool of glitch culture. His practice spans real-time generative systems and the on-chain PXL ecosystem.

larva-labs.bio: Larva Labs (Matt Hall and John Watkinson) created CryptoPunks in 2017: ten thousand algorithmically generated characters, claimed for free, that became the archetype for on-chain identity, helping pioneer on-chain generative art and sparking the NFT movement.

operator.bio: Operator (Ania Catherine and Dejha Ti) pair choreography with code, building conceptual systems that join environments, technology, and the body. Human Unreadable, winner of the 2023 Lumen Prize for generative art, derives each work from an on-chain choreographic sequence, released in stages from abstraction to live performance.

refik-anadol.bio: Refik Anadol renders data as moving image, using machine learning as his medium. Unsupervised was acquired into MoMA's permanent collection in 2023, a landmark for generative-AI art. Winds of Yawanawá and Biome Lumina extended the practice into living, dynamic works: the throughline that led to Dataland, his Museum of AI Arts in Los Angeles.

sam-spratt.bio: Sam Spratt brings old-world craftsmanship to digital tools, carrying the vocabulary of classical portraiture into a blockchain-native practice. His ongoing Luci mythology, extended across successive chapters, treats the chain as a medium for participatory authorship and long-form narrative.

tyler-hobbs-and-dandelion-wist.bio: A collaboration between Tyler Hobbs and Dandelion Wist, QQL is a generative art algorithm that invites collectors to compose their own outputs, blending the artist's system with the collector's creative input.

tyler-hobbs.bio: Tyler Hobbs is among the defining figures of long-form generative art. His practice pairs precise algorithmic design with the chaos that emerges from human input and computational surprise. Fidenza, his 999-output Art Blocks Curated collection, extended his earlier flow-field studies into a fully realized algorithmic language.

xcopy.bio: XCOPY is a pseudonymous artist whose glitch aesthetic has defined the visual language of crypto-native art. Themes of societal decay, mortality, and tech-mediated apathy are central to his work, which confronts viewers with a raw critique of the internet era.

ack-editions.curatorNote: Two works by a.c.k. on his 1/1 contract, both drawing on the botanical vocabulary that runs through his practice.

cope-salada.curatorNote: Cope Salada is XCOPY at his most compressed: a single-piece contract that reads as a distilled statement of his 2023 register.

cryptopunks.curatorNote:
CryptoPunks established the cultural foundation for everything that followed in NFTs: at once a historical artifact and an on-chain identity standard.

Hivemind's Punk holding is built around a core trait spine (Big Beard, Hoodie, Cap Forward, Tiara), supported by a selection of Punks with aesthetic accessories.

dataland-biome-lumina.curatorNote:
Biome Lumina opens a new register for AI-driven art: works that breathe and respond rather than resolve as static outputs. As one of the founding works of Dataland, it helps open the first museum devoted to AI art.

Hivemind's eighteen pieces span twelve species clusters across flora and fungi, including a Rare Mystical Variant.

day-gardens.curatorNote: Day Gardens is Tyler Hobbs' 2025 study in observational algorithmic painting: a return to the painterly with the discipline of his generative practice intact. Hivemind's triptych spans the series' tonal range, from restrained to expressive.

fidenza.curatorNote:
Fidenza is the definitive Art Blocks collection and a cornerstone of the digital art canon: 999 flow-field compositions that set the benchmark for long-form on-chain generative art.

Hivemind's Fidenza holding is built around core traits that show the range of Hobbs' algorithm, including White Mono, Spiral, Black & White and Micro, and is intended to read as a coherent sub-collection rather than a representative sample.

grifters.curatorNote:
Grifters is XCOPY's pinnacle PFP collection: a generative cast of 666 characters minted December 2021 in his cynical visual language.

As top holder, Hivemind has assembled color-complete sets across Wretch, Shady and G to the M Grifters, anchored by ROTTEN, one of only three Legendary outputs in the collection.

harbor-scene.curatorNote:
Harbor Scene is a contemporary response to American Impressionism: three unique iterations from Hobbs' algorithm engaging John Henry Twachtman's unfinished Harbor Scene, housed in LACMA's permanent collection.

Hivemind acquired Harbor Scene #2 through LACMA and Cactoid Labs' Remembrance of Things Future, extending the fund's existing Hobbs 1/1 position.

her-favorite-flowers.curatorNote:
Her favorite flowers is an early 2023 study in textured blossom work: the seed of what has defined a.c.k.'s recent practice.

Hivemind holds one of two works on the contract. kissed by the Moonlight sits alongside the Piano Blossoms suite, extending a.c.k.'s botanical arc.

human-unreadable.curatorNote:
Human Unreadable maps choreography onto generative code across a three-act structure, culminating in a live performance.

Hivemind holds eighteen generative works from the series, alongside the only large-scale X-Ray Machine sculpture Operator will produce: a physical extension of the collection's material vocabulary.

context.label: Read the X-Ray Machine announcement on X

lights.curatorNote:
Lights is a collection of 1/1 real-time animations running on a custom smart contract written for on-chain artworks, through which the code can be delivered without any third-party server involvement.

Hivemind holds Raster und Spektrum: a unique, deterministic, fully on-chain real-time animation, written in WebGL2 and JavaScript with no external dependencies. It was unveiled at Art Basel Miami 2025.

lightyears.curatorNote: Hivemind holds six Light Years from Dmitri Cherniak's collaboration with The Estate of László Moholy-Nagy: an on-chain collection where each token claims a companion silver-gelatin print, bridging Bauhaus experimentation with generative code.

context.label: Read the Hivemind acquisition post on X

masks-of-luci.curatorNote: Hivemind holds eighteen masks: two complete sets across all eight categories, plus two Council Masks. This represents a substantial position in the Luci ecosystem and strong belief in Spratt's talent and worldbuilding.

meebit.curatorNote: Larva Labs's 3D counterpart to CryptoPunks: a companion holding that carries the same voxel-native provenance into three dimensions.

notable-pepes.curatorNote: A selection from the Rare Pepe canon: on-chain cards that pre-date most NFT infrastructure and anchor the meme in verifiable provenance.

piano-blossoms.curatorNote: Hivemind holds the complete suite from a.c.k.'s Piano Blossoms auction, his first solo exhibition, in Amsterdam with Avant Arte, October 2024. The contract also carries a sixth piece, a.c.k.'s post-auction self-portrait.

context.label: Read the Hivemind acquisition post on X

pxl-dex.curatorNote: PXL DEX is Kim Asendorf's most rigorous statement on pixel-as-token: 256 on-chain animations where each pixel is a finite, tradable unit. Hivemind holds five, a strong position in Asendorf's ongoing PXL ecosystem alongside PXL POD.

pxl-pod.curatorNote: Hivemind holds eight Single Pods alongside two Duo Pods, the latter developed for Asendorf's solo presentation at Art Basel Hong Kong 2026.

qql.curatorNote:
QQL turned generative art into a participatory medium: collectors compose their own outputs from Hobbs and Wist's algorithm, becoming co-authors of the work.

Hivemind holds two minted artworks, both composed and minted by Hobbs, alongside ten unspent Mint Passes: a position on both authorship and reserve.

repeat-as-necessary.curatorNote:
Repeat as Necessary continues Operator's study of performance as an owned medium and the body as algorithmic input: a meditation on breath, presence, and dissolution.

Hivemind holds four of forty outputs, one from each of the series' four chapters.

ringers.curatorNote: Hivemind holds thirty-six Ringers, among the largest single-collector positions in the project, curated across notable traits including Red Background, Yellow Background and Bullseye. Five pieces previously resided in 3AC's Starry Night collection alongside The Goose.

skulls-of-luci.curatorNote: Hivemind holds two of the fifty Skulls of Luci, alongside their two Council Mask counterparts, and with them two seats on the Council of Luci.

context.label: Read the Hivemind acquisition post on X

superrare-beeple.curatorNote:
Beeple's TIME: The Future of Business captured a distinct cultural moment: a digital-native artist collaborating with a legacy magazine to reflect on a world in acceleration. Created in Cinema 4D and OctaneRender, the composition embeds Beeple's own design interface into the work, collapsing process and product into one image.

Hivemind holds the 1/1 NFT of the TIME cover, among the most-cited works of Beeple's SuperRare practice.

superrare-dmitri-cherniak.curatorNote:
Coded in JavaScript and rendered as SVG in the browser, A Slight Lack of Symmetry Can Cause So Much Pain is a geometric exploration of looped strings and pegs that pre-dates Cherniak's Art Blocks practice.

Hivemind holds two of the four algorithmic 1/1s, both acquired through Sotheby's 3AC 'Grails' sale from the disbanded Starry Night Capital collection.
superrare-dmitri-cherniak.essayTitle: Inside the Collection: A Slight Lack of Symmetry

superrare-xcopy.curatorNote:
XCOPY's art helped define the visual language of CryptoArt through his glitch-heavy, nihilistic aesthetic and his sustained study of death, identity and digital decay. The 149 1/1s released on SuperRare between 2018 and 2023, spanning figurative, commentary and abstract registers, form the spine of his practice.

Hivemind holds five XCOPY SuperRare 1/1s, predominantly from his COVID-era work, when colors sharpened and lines cleaned. Dankrupt, Some Other Asshole and THE FUD are among XCOPY's most notable figurative works; Dankrupt served as his X profile picture throughout 2025.

synthetic-dreams.curatorNote:
Created by Anadol for the Google Quantum Summer Symposium 2021, each work is generated from quantum random states. The series documents an early collaboration between a major artist and frontier quantum-computing research.

Hivemind holds twelve Synthetic Dreams, part of a substantial position in Refik Anadol.

tyler-hobbs.curatorNote:
Hobbs' 1/1 contract features four works (Return Zero, Elektroanima, One One Overflow and Days Fade) that exemplify his merging of manual and computational processes, whether through hand-drawn curves or algorithmic dot placement.

Hivemind holds three of four works on the contract. One One Overflow embraces algorithmic unpredictability and imperfection; Elektroanima explores the interplay between hand-drawn mark and algorithmic precision; and Return Zero [Blue] establishes the flowing curves and geometric shapes that would later evolve into Fidenza.

winds-of-yawanawa.curatorNote:
Winds of Yawanawá pairs Refik Anadol Studio's data-painting practice with the Yawanawá community of the Brazilian Amazon, channeling weather data from the tribe's village through the visual language of young Yawanawá artists.

Hivemind is the largest single holder of the 1,000-piece series, with a core focus on the Flowers, Scorching and Freezing traits.
winds-of-yawanawa.essayTitle: Inside the Collection: Winds of Yawanawá

x0x.curatorNote: Asendorf's X0X extends the pixel investigations of PXL DEX and PXL POD into a shorter-form 1/1 statement.
